reasoning: As of early June 2025, all evidence strongly indicates that neither Bulgargaz nor BOTAŞ will officially announce the cancellation of the gas agreement before July 1, 2025. The contract lacks a termination clause, and Bulgarian officials (including the Energy Minister and MPs in mid-to-late May 2025) have publicly stated that 'this agreement cannot be terminated by anybody.' Terminating it would trigger an immediate an exorbitant BGN 3-4.2 billion penalty for Bulgaria, which Bulgargaz cannot afford as it is on the verge of bankruptcy. Instead, Bulgaria is attempting to renegotiate terms by hiring a Swiss law firm and conducting ongoing talks with BOTAŞ, which offered contract amendment options in late March 2025. Neither side benefits from a unilateral explicit 'cancellation' in the span of the next 25 days, as Bulgaria wants to avoid immediate massive penalties, and BOTAŞ wants to preserve a highly lucrative long-term contract or enforce payments through arbitration if necessary. The timeline is simply too short and the structural incentives are entirely opposed to an outright cancellation announcement.
